sector: Technology / Power Semiconductors & Modules (point-of-load power delivery)
bull_thesis: Vicor owns the foundational IP for Vertical Power Delivery (VPD) / Factorized Power Architecture, the only efficient way to deliver 1,500W+ at sub-1.6V to a next-gen AI GPU through the last 1.5mm of the power chain. The Feb-2025 ITC Final Determination (U.S. Patents 9,516,761 / 9,166,481) and Limited Exclusion Order barring import of unlicensed computing systems converted a sleepy module maker into a product-plus-toll-booth: leading OEMs and hyperscalers were forced to license, contributing ~$300M through 2026; FY2025 royalty hit $57.4M (+23%). The commercial signal is now loud: Q1'26 revenue $113.0M (+20.2% YoY), gross margin 55.2% (+800bps YoY), backlog $300.6M (+70% QoQ, +75% YoY), book-to-bill >2.0, FY26 guide ~$570M. The balance sheet is a fortress — $402.8M cash, effectively debt-free, ~$119M FY25 FCF — so the capacity ramp ($1B Fab One scalable to $1.5B, second fab via building acquisition) is self-funding. 2nd-gen VPD (3 A/mm2, up to 40x current multiplication, 1.5mm package) is positioned directly under Nvidia Vera Rubin-class GPUs, the fastest-growing, most physically inescapable layer of the AI build-out. If the backlog converts and VPD becomes the standard point-of-load topology for Rubin/Kyber (2027), Vicor earns a durable, high-margin, architecture-locked franchise that the multiple is reaching for.
bear_thesis: The stock has quintupled in twelve months (+494% TTM, +145-179% YTD) to ~108x forward P/E and >20x sales — the AI-power optionality is already in the price, and then some. The 2025 earnings quality is litigation-heavy: a one-time $45M settlement and a +23% royalty step-up carried the headline +26% growth, while the *product* business grew far slower; Q1'26's $0.44 GAAP EPS was flattered by a $27.3M tax benefit (pre-tax operating income only ~$16.9M). 'Nearly 100%-margin' licensing inadvertently reveals the underlying product gross margin is far below the blended 55%. Litigation cuts both ways — Vicor PAID SynQor $28.6M in March 2026 — and ~$300M of licensing is framed 'through 2026' with no disclosed post-2026 run-rate; new deals are explicitly excluded pending a 2027 ITC resolution, so the royalty annuity is contingent on future legal outcomes. The load-bearing ITC catalyst already FIRED (Feb-2025) — the forced-licensing re-rate is spent. CEO Vinciarelli's public polemic against the competing 800V-to-6V architecture is a tell that the point-of-load topology is contested, not settled; if hyperscalers standardize on a non-Vicor power architecture, the VPD product TAM narrows even with the patents intact. Founder/dual-class control limits external governance check. A name priced for three years of flawless execution is asymmetric to the downside on any conversion stumble.
sop_bull: ~$330-360/share. Whole-company intrinsic on a high-quality IP+ramp base. Bull frame: FY26 ~$570M rev converting toward a ~$700M+ run-rate as backlog ($300.6M, B:B>2) ships; assume a mature blended operating margin of ~28-30% on $700M = ~$200M operating income, ~$165M net at a normalized tax rate. On ~46M diluted shares, ~$3.60 EPS run-rate. A premium AI-exposed IP compounder multiple of ~90-95x forward (in line with where MPWR-class scarcity names have traded at peak AI enthusiasm) = ~$325-340, plus ~$9/share net cash ($403M / 46M) = ~$335-350. Add an IP-annuity overlay (perpetual high-margin royalty stream renewed via the 2027 ITC leverage) worth ~$10-15/share = ~$345-360.
sop_bear: ~$110-140/share. Strip the windfalls and value the durable productive franchise. Bear frame: normalize OUT the one-time $45M settlement and treat royalty as a decaying annuity (no guaranteed post-2026 renewal). Product+recurring-royalty earning power ~$0.90-1.20/share normalized (FY25 ex-tax-benefit, ex-settlement run-rate). On a still-rich-but-defensible ~40-45x growth-semi multiple (POWI/MPWR cohort de-rated) = ~$45-55 on operations, + ~$9 net cash, + a conservative ~$50-70/share for the genuinely-validated IP/VPD optionality and backlog conversion = ~$110-140. This is what's left if the architecture war goes against VPD or the multiple compresses to the cohort.
sop_breakdown: Base case blends: (1) Productive franchise — FY26 $570M -> ~$650M run-rate at ~26% op margin, ~$150M operating income, ~$120M net on ~46M shares ~= $2.60 normalized EPS at a ~60-65x AI-IP-premium-but-not-peak multiple = ~$160-170. (2) Net cash ~$9/share (~$403M debt-free). (3) IP/royalty annuity overlay, haircut for post-2026 uncertainty and the offsetting SynQor payment = ~$35-50/share. Sum ~$205-230. The wide bull/bear gap ($110 vs $350) is itself the verdict: the value is real but the price ($328) sits above even a generous base-case SoP, so the market is paying for the bull tail, not the base.
risk_reward: Unfavorable. At ~$328 vs base-case SoP ~$215, downside-to-fair-value is ~-34% and downside-to-bear-break ~-58 to -66%; upside-to-bull ~+5 to +10%. Roughly 6:1 AGAINST on a base-case lens; the asymmetry is to the downside because the run already happened.
peer_comps_note: True scale-out peers within Basket D (point-of-load / AI power-delivery semis & modules): Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) — the closest AI-power comp, trading ~52x NTM EV/EBITDA and ~59x forward P/E (June 2026, the richest in the cohort), a far larger ($30B+) and more diversified franchise with direct GPU VRM share; Power Integrations (POWI) — high-voltage / GaN, lower-growth, value-cohort multiple; Navitas (NVTS) — GaN/SiC pure-play, speculative, benchmarked against MPWR/POWI/Infineon; Infineon (IFX) and onsemi (ON) — broad-line SiC/power, ~15-25x. Texas Instruments (TXN) ~24x and Analog Devices (ADI) ~20x EV/EBITDA anchor the analog floor; Nvidia ~17x and Broadcom ~19x show even the AI hyperscalers trade FAR cheaper than this cohort. RANKING: VICR is the MOST expensive in the entire complex (~108x forward P/E, >20x sales) — richer than even MPWR — on the SMALLEST revenue base ($570M FY26E vs MPWR multi-billion). It ranks #1 on AI-capex purity/IP-criticality and gross-margin trajectory (55%), but LAST on valuation and scale, and its returns are partly litigation-sourced rather than purely productive. The peer set itself has re-rated to AI-bubble multiples; VICR sits at the extreme tail of that re-rating.
role_in_scaleout: Vicor is the IP owner of the 'last 1.5mm' — Vertical Power Delivery places its modules directly beneath 1,500W+ AI GPUs to deliver sub-1.6V core power at ~50% lower loss than horizontal delivery, the most physically inescapable point in the rack power chain; demand is highly durable so long as VPD remains the standard point-of-load topology for Rubin-class accelerators, but it is contingent on winning the architecture war, not guaranteed by the patents alone.
consensus_delta: Wall Street is NOT looking the wrong way here in the contrarian sense the strategy hunts for — it is looking directly at this name and has bid it to ~108x forward earnings, a consensus target ~$282 (Needham $350), and a +145-179% YTD move. The expectations-arbitrage edge is INVERTED: rather than the street under-pricing a hidden catalyst, the street has fully priced (arguably over-priced) the AI-power-monopoly thesis. The one thing consensus may be soft-pedaling is earnings QUALITY — the $27.3M tax benefit inside Q1'26 EPS and the $45M one-time FY25 settlement mean the productive run-rate is lower than the GAAP optics, and the 'through 2026' framing of the ~$300M licensing leaves the post-2026 royalty annuity undefined. But that is a downside-revealing nuance, not a hidden bull catalyst. There is no mispriced asymmetric upside left to arb.
valley_of_death: Near-term (3-9mo) risks: (1) Earnings-quality reset — the next prints lap a $27.3M tax benefit and a non-repeating $45M settlement; reported growth and EPS could decelerate sharply on a clean comp even as the business grows, a classic multiple-compression trigger at 108x. (2) Capex inflection — the $1B->$1.5B capacity claim and a second fab imply a step-up in spend that will pressure the currently-pristine FCF as it lands. (3) Architecture-war headline risk — any hyperscaler signal favoring a competing 800V-to-6V / non-VPD point-of-load topology (the architecture Vinciarelli publicly disparaged) would hit the product-TAM narrative hard. (4) AI-capex is the single shared macro factor under this entire basket; per the regime doc it is ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth and the dominant two-sided risk — any hyperscaler capex guide-down de-grosses the whole power-semi cohort simultaneously, and a 108x name has the most multiple to lose.
positioning_washout: This is a momentum/growth-owned name (+494% TTM), not a yield or value base, so there is no dividend-cut or passive-yield washout mechanic. The washout risk is the reverse: a crowded fast-money/AI-thematic shareholder base that would liquidate MECHANICALLY on the first earnings-quality miss or AI-capex scare, with little valuation support beneath ($403M net cash is only ~$9/share against a $328 print — ~3% of the market cap). The absence of a value floor means a de-rating could be violent. No forced-buyer offset of note.
forcing_function: The hard re-rating catalysts are mostly BEHIND the stock: the Feb-2025 ITC Final Determination / Limited Exclusion Order already FIRED and drove the licensing settlements and the bulk of the re-rate. Forward dated events that could move it: (1) the 2027 ITC litigation resolution that gates NEW licensing deals (a real but distant binary, and two-sided); (2) Vera Rubin production ramp milestones (air-cooled samples mid-2026, liquid-cooled Ultra late-2026, full 800VDC with Kyber 2027) — design-win confirmations could extend the story but are largely anticipated; (3) the clean-comp quarters in 2H-2026 when the tax-benefit and settlement tailwinds roll off — the most likely de-rating forcing function. None of these is an undiscovered asymmetric long catalyst.
moderator_conclusion: Applying the 2026-06 regime (complacent melt-up, AI-capex as the load-bearing wall ~40% of S&P EPS growth, enforce resolution-driver independence): Vicor is a genuinely excellent franchise — court-validated foundational IP at the most physically inescapable point in the AI power chain, a WIDE and WIDENING moat (55% GM, ~100%-margin royalties, $403M net cash, ~$119M FCF, backlog +70% QoQ at B:B>2). The Interrogator's 4/5 credibility and STRENGTHENING trajectory are warranted. BUT the CRO's job is timing and margin of safety, and on both this is a PASS. (1) The load-bearing ITC catalyst already FIRED in Feb-2025 — per the regime mandate I PENALIZE already-fired / already-ran catalysts; the re-rate is spent and the stock has quintupled on it. (2) At ~108x forward P/E and >20x sales the stock trades ABOVE even a generous base-case SoP (~$215 vs $328) — risk/reward is ~6:1 against, with no value floor (net cash is only ~3% of cap). (3) Earnings quality is litigation/tax-flattered, and the cleanest 2H-2026 comps are a de-rating forcing function, not an upside one. (4) Critically for book independence: VICR's entire thesis hinges on the SAME single shared macro factor — AI-capex durability — as the rest of Basket D and much of the index; it adds concentration, not diversification. The value case absent the AI overlay is weak (you do not pay 108x for a $570M-revenue power-module maker whose productive margins are far below the royalty-blended 55%), so value_conviction is also 2. Verdict C / conviction 2. This is a buy-the-dip-someday name, not an entry here; the architecture is real but the price front-ran two-to-three years of perfect execution and a contested point-of-load architecture war. Watchlist only on a meaningful AI-capex flush that resets the multiple toward the SoP.
interrogator_dossier:
# Forensic Dossier — Vicor Corporation (NASDAQ: VICR)

*Scale-out Basket D (PWR-SEMI). Power modules / 800V-relevant point-of-load power delivery. Prepared as Claude Opus 4.8, Interrogator seat. Data live-verified June 2026; no transcript bundle was provided, so financials and management commentary were sourced via web (Q1 2026 earnings call, FY2025 10-K/8-K, ITC filings).*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

Vicor's story has undergone one of the cleanest narrative transformations in the power-semi complex over the last two years. The company that for a decade was a sleepy, capacity-constrained, gross-margin-volatile maker of high-density power modules — perennially 'about to inflect' on a 48V data-center transition that kept slipping — has been re-cast as the owner of the foundational IP for Vertical Power Delivery (VPD) / Factorized Power Architecture, the physical method by which the last 1.5mm of power reaches a 1,500W+ AI GPU at sub-1.6V core voltage.

Three legs: (1) IP awakening (2024->Feb-2025) — the Feb-2025 ITC Final Determination validating U.S. Patents 9,516,761 and 9,166,481 and issuing a Limited Exclusion Order, converting a product company into product-plus-toll-booth. (2) Royalty harvest (2025) — FY2025 royalty $57.4M (+23.2% YoY) plus a one-time $45M settlement, lifting total FY2025 revenue +26.1% to $452.7M, ~$300M cumulative licensing through 2026. (3) VPD product ramp (2026->) — 2nd-gen VPD at 3 A/mm2, up to 40x current multiplication, 1.5mm package, under Vera Rubin GPUs; Q1'26 revenue $113.0M (+20.2% YoY), backlog $300.6M (+70% QoQ, +75% YoY), book-to-bill >2.0, FY26 guide ~$570M. Quietly dropped: capacity/margin hand-wringing. Introduced: a polemical posture — Vinciarelli dismissing the competing '800V-to-6V' architecture as 'ill-conceived' / 'structurally misguided.'

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

Demand capacity-constrained, B:B>2.0 — CORROBORATED (backlog $300.6M +70% QoQ, FY26 guide $570M). GM expanding structurally — PARTIALLY (55.2% vs 47.2% YoY +800bps, but 55.4%->55.2% sequential, Q2'25 spiked 65.3% on royalty mix). Licensing ~100% margin — CORROBORATED and crux: profit quality is litigation-derived. Net income inflecting — OVERSTATED (Q1'26 $0.44 EPS includes a $27.3M tax benefit; pre-tax operating income only ~$16.9M). Fortress balance sheet — CORROBORATED ($402.8M cash, debt-free, FY25 OCF $139.5M, capex $20.3M, FCF ~$119M). Capacity to $1.5B — PLAUSIBLE/UNPROVEN. The divergence that matters: the AI-monopoly narrative is richer than today's productive financials — FY25's +26% was carried by a $45M one-time settlement and a +23% royalty step-up; the product business grew far slower.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

Rising — assertively. Vinciarelli's Q1'26 register is the most offensive in company history, publicly disparaging competing architectures ('inadequate current density and stacked packages that are not mechanically and thermally adept'), reframing scarcity as pricing power. Risk: founder-controlled over-reach, and the polemic against 800V-to-6V is a tell that the architecture debate is live and not settled in Vicor's favor industry-wide.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

Medium. Q1'26 beat ($0.44-0.52 vs $0.37-0.41), FY26 ~$570M concrete. But the pre-2025 record was repeated 48V-inflection deferrals; the credibility upgrade rests on the exogenous ITC outcome, not commercial execution. Royalty/settlement income is lumpy and partly non-recurring ($45M settlement won't repeat; Vicor PAID SynQor $28.6M in Mar-2026). Product ramp unproven at new scale. Score: Medium, trending up only if backlog converts.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

Litigation dependence (royalty vs product split — bundled by management); customer-concentration / design-win confirmation (Vera Rubin VPD inferred from import-leverage, not disclosed contracts); capacity/capex (a $1.5B fab implies a future capex step-up not yet in cash flows); royalty durability post-2026 (the ~$300M is framed 'through 2026' with no defined run-rate after, and new deals await a 2027 ITC resolution).

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

GREEN: net-cash debt-free ($403M), ~$119M FCF; court-validated foundational IP (ITC LEO); backlog +70% QoQ B:B>2; +800bps YoY GM. RED: earnings quality (Q1'26 EPS flattered by $27.3M tax benefit; FY25 by $45M settlement); valuation (~108x fwd P/E, >20x sales, +494% TTM); litigation two-way (paid SynQor $28.6M); unsettled architecture debate (800V-to-6V); founder/dual-class governance.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

The reframing of 'capacity-constrained' from apology into selectivity lever is the key tonal tell (prices scarcity vs chases volume). Guidance EXCLUDES new licensing deals pending the 2027 ITC resolution — both a sandbag and a flag that royalty durability hinges on future legal outcomes. The pivot back to product (VPD specs) after a year leaning on royalties suggests management knows the litigation windfall isn't a permanent base and is racing to convert IP leverage into shipped silicon before the architecture war is decided.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

Trajectory: STRENGTHENING — off a base whose quality is mixed (litigation-heavy 2025, tax-flattered Q1'26) and whose price has already discounted the strengthening. The single most important thing to weigh: how much AI-power-delivery optionality is already in a ~108x forward P/E / >20x sales stock that quintupled in twelve months. Vicor owns real, court-validated foundational IP at the most physically inescapable point in the AI power chain, a wide-feeling moat — but the productive ramp that would EARN the multiple is still ahead and partly hostage to an unsettled point-of-load architecture debate and future litigation. A great franchise at a price that has front-run two-to-three years of perfect execution. Moat: intangible (patents) + regulatory-license (ITC LEO) + switching-cost (architecture lock-in). Width WIDE on returns evidence but legally rather than purely commercially sourced. Trend WIDENING. Secular force: AI scale-out is a tailwind; only terminal risk is architectural displacement (competitive, not secular-decline). Secular threat: manageable. This is not a fear-print value name — it is a richly-priced IP/growth name where the risk is multiple compression on any execution stumble.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: WIDE | MOAT_TREND: WIDENING | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
